case_name:
(b)The maintenance of professional standards.This is referred
to inDentice v The  Valuers  Registration  Board[1991] NZHC 1689;[1992]  1 NZLR  720, 
724-725  and inB v Medical  Council  of New  Zealand[2005]  3 NZLR 
p.180  L.48  High  Court, Elias J.

summary.brief:
(b)The maintenance of professional standards.This is referred
to inDentice v The  Valuers  Registration  Board[1991] NZHC 1689;[1992]  1 NZLR  720, 
724-725  and inB v Medical  Council  of New  Zealand[2005]  3 NZLR 
p.180  L.48  High  Court, Elias J.. Medical malpractice case involving oncology.

evidence_index.text: Dr Stubbs stated he accepted “absolutely” the decision of the Tribunal which found he had no right to proceed with Mr N’s surgery once he knew the risks to his patient were substantially greater than those he had previously outlined to him.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr Stubbs

evidence_index.text: The Tribunal had found the charge proved, the presumption of innocence no longer applied.
evidence_index.speaker: Tribunal

evidence_index.text: Dr Stubbs failed to inform adequately his patient in that case of the possible serious complications and risks and adverse effects of performing a revision gastric bypass so that the patient was unable to make an informed choice as to whether to undertake the surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Tribunal

evidence_index.text: The conduct alleged in paragraphs 1 to 2 either separately or cumulatively amounts to professional misconduct.
evidence_index.speaker: Tribunal

evidence_index.text: The Tribunal’s findings gave rise to serious concerns about the quality of the care provided by Dr Stubbs and therefore raised serious public safety issues.
evidence_index.speaker: Tribunal

evidence_index.text: The Tribunal now makes orders prohibiting, on a permanent basis, the publication of the names of the following persons who were referred to at the substantive hearing.
evidence_index.speaker: Tribunal

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient was scheduled for elective gastric bypass surgery.

focused_exam.system: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Patient scheduled for elective gastric bypass surgery.

requestables.name: Liver Function Tests
requestables.description: Assessment of liver function prior to surgery

reveal.clinical_significance: Increased risk of complications during surgery
reveal.decision_impact: Should have prompted further investigation or discussion with the patient

timeline_phases.name: Pre-operative Assessment
timeline_phases.description: Patient assessed for elective gastric bypass surgery

timeline_phases.name: Pre-surgery Workup
timeline_phases.description: Review of pre-operative tests and decision to proceed with surgery

key_events.event: Decision to proceed with surgery despite abnormal liver function

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient scheduled for elective gastric bypass surgery with abnormal liver function tests
decision_points.prompt: What should be done upon discovering abnormal liver function tests?

options.description: Proceed with surgery without informing the patient
options.clinical_reasoning: Defendant believed it was in the patient's best interest to proceed

options.description: Inform the patient of the abnormal results and discuss increased risks
options.clinical_reasoning: Patient autonomy and informed consent are critical

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant proceeded with surgery without informing the patient
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Believed it was in the patient's best interest

expected_action_court.description: Patient should have been informed of the abnormal liver function and associated risks
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Informed consent is a fundamental patient right

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to obtain informed consent is a breach of patient rights
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Patient should have been informed and consent obtained

patient_outcome.description: Patient faced increased risk of complications due to surgery with abnormal liver function

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to obtain informed consent
Causation established - increased risk not communicated to patient

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to inform patient of abnormal liver function and associated risks
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Patient should have been informed and consent obtained

treatments.outcome: Increased risk due to abnormal liver function
